question_text: A food scientist measures that 100 grams of bread releases 1200 kJ of energy when completely burned in a calorimeter. Based on this result, approximately how much energy would 25 grams of the same bread release?
explanation: This is a simple proportion problem: if 100 g releases 1200 kJ, then 25 g (which is one-quarter of 100 g) releases one-quarter of the energy. 1200 kJ ÷ 4 = 300 kJ. The chemical energy content per gram of bread remains constant.
